understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each KA59 level by placing all selectable pieces onto the goal tiles marked '+' at the same time; if there were multiple '+' tiles, every one had to be occupied simultaneously for the level to register WIN, and completing all levels would win the whole game within the per-level step budgets.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) per action, subject to collisions: walls generally blocked motion, and pushable blocks could be pushed if immediately adjacent in the move direction and there was space for them to slide. Levels could contain multiple selectables; CLICK with a target_position in the same pixel-coordinate system as player.position switched control to the selectable at that position. The movement_blocked flags were only hints about adjacency and did not guarantee success/failure, so actions sometimes needed to be tested against boundaries/objects to learn their behavior.
